data.sentence: Hi Stephanie, thanks for making time today — I know you've got a lot going on.
Yeah, it's been a week, honestly. But yeah, I wanted to have this conversation so, here we are.

I appreciate it. So, um, I wanted to reach out specifically because your renewal is coming up at the end of next month and I just — I want to make sure we're in a good place and that you're getting value out of the platform.
Aisha, I'll be straight with you. We're not in a great place right now. And I think you probably know why.
The outage.

Yes. The outage. We had six hours — six hours — where we had zero visibility into our threat environment. Do you understand what that means for a retail operation our size? We're processing transactions, we've got customer data, we've got POS systems across 200-plus locations. Six hours is not a blip, Aisha. That's a liability.

I completely understand, and I want to be very clear — I'm not here to minimize what happened. The Aegis Detect outage on March 10th was serious, and I know it hit a lot of our customers hard, but frankly, a customer like Nova with your threat surface, I — yeah, I know it was especially painful.
Painful is one word for it. Our CISO was asking me why we were paying for threat monitoring that wasn't monitoring anything. And honestly I didn't have a good answer.

That's a really hard position to be in and I'm sorry you were put there. Can I — can I share what we've actually done in response? Because I don't want this to feel like a PR answer but I do think the remediation work is meaningful.
Go ahead.

So the root cause was a single point of failure in our event ingestion pipeline. Essentially when one node got overwhelmed it cascaded. What we've done since is deploy redundant processing nodes across the pipeline and implement a circuit breaker pattern so that if one component starts failing it doesn't bring down the whole system. We've also increased our alerting thresholds so our on-call team gets flagged much earlier.

Okay, but — and I say this with all due respect — why wasn't that architecture in place to begin with? That's not a novel engineering concept. Circuit breakers, redundancy, these are table stakes for a platform that's supposed to be enterprise-grade.
You're right. And I don't have a satisfying answer for that. It's a gap that shouldn't have existed and it did and we're — we're accountable for that.

I appreciate the honesty at least. Look, we've been with Aegis for — what is it, almost three years now? This isn't coming from a place of just wanting to switch for the sake of switching. But I do have to make decisions that I can defend to my board and to our security team.
Of course. And that's exactly the kind of conversation I want to have. What — what are the specific things that would need to be true for you to feel confident going into a renewal?

Well, a few things. One is obviously the reliability concern we just talked about. Two is — and this has been a slow-burn frustration for us — our compliance reporting situation. We are under PCI DSS obligations, we've got some HIPAA-adjacent data from our pharmacy locations, and right now generating those reports out of Aegis Comply is... it's manual, it's painful, it takes my team days to pull together what should be a few clicks.
Okay, that's — that's actually really important feedback and I want to come back to that specifically because there's something relevant there. What's the third thing?
The third thing is that we've been talking to SentinelShield. And I want to be transparent about that because I think you deserve to know.
I appreciate you telling me that. How far along are those conversations?

We've had two demos. We haven't asked for a formal proposal yet, but we're close. Their threat detection coverage looks solid and they made a pretty compelling case for their uptime SLAs, which given everything is — yeah, it's on our radar.

Understood. And I'm not going to sit here and trash SentinelShield, that's not my style. But I do want to make sure you have the full picture on where we are and where we're going, because I think some of what you've described — especially on the compliance side — is actually about to change significantly.
What do you mean?

So Aegis Comply v2 is launching on April 7th — which I know is after your renewal window opens, so I want to be upfront about that — but it includes on-demand reporting for PCI DSS, HIPAA, SOC 2, and ISO 27001. Multi-framework, no manual aggregation, you can pull a report in real time. The workflow your team is dealing with today basically goes away.
On-demand meaning... what exactly? Because I've heard 'on-demand' from vendors before and it means 'we queued your report and it'll be ready in four hours.'

Ha — fair. No, I mean genuinely on-demand. The data's already being ingested and mapped against the control frameworks continuously, so when you click generate, you're getting a live report. We're talking minutes, not hours. I can get you into a preview environment if that would help you evaluate it before the decision.
That would actually be useful. My compliance lead has been pulling her hair out over this so — yeah, let's do that.

Great, I'll set that up. Now I also — going back to the SentinelShield piece — I want to ask you something directly. Is the Detect outage the primary driver here or is it more of a cumulative frustration thing? Because those are kind of different problems.

Honestly? It's both. The outage was the thing that made this feel urgent. But if I'm being real with you, we've had some grumbles about support response times, about the Detect dashboard being a bit clunky for our team, and the compliance stuff has been a background annoyance for a while. So it all kind of... crystallized.

That makes a lot of sense. And I don't want to dismiss any of those. The dashboard feedback — can you tell me more about what specifically feels clunky? Is it the alert triage flow, the filtering, or something else?

Um, it's mostly the alert volume and the signal-to-noise ratio. We're getting flagged on things that are clearly not threats for our environment and then when something real comes through it can get buried. My SOC analyst — she actually flagged this to your support team back in January and I don't think she got a satisfying resolution.
I'm going to pull that ticket after this call and personally follow up. That should not have been left unresolved for two months. I'm sorry about that.

I'd appreciate that. Look, Aisha, I want to be clear — I'm not trying to be difficult here. I actually like working with you and I think the Aegis platform has genuinely helped us. But I have to be able to justify this to leadership and right now the story has some holes in it.

I hear you. And I think that's completely fair. So let me propose this — I'll set up the Comply v2 preview with you and your compliance lead, I'll personally chase down that support ticket and get you a resolution, and I'd also like to put together a written summary of the Detect remediation steps with our updated SLA commitments so you have something concrete to bring to your CISO. Does that seem like a reasonable set of next steps?

Yeah, that seems reasonable. I'd also want to have a conversation about whether there's any flexibility on the commercial side given... everything. I'm not saying that's a dealbreaker but it's something I'd need to at least explore.

Absolutely, and I'll loop in my account executive on that piece. I want to make sure if we're talking numbers we're having the right conversation. I don't want to throw something out without being able to back it up properly.
That works. Okay. I think — I came into this call pretty frustrated, so I'll give you credit, this was a more useful conversation than I expected.
I'm glad. And Stephanie, genuinely — I want to earn the renewal, not just retain the contract. There's a difference and I take that seriously.
I appreciate that. Alright, send me the calendar invite for the Comply preview and I'll get Sarah from our compliance team on it.
Will do. I'll have something out to you by end of day Monday. Thanks again for being so direct with me today, it actually helps me help you.
Yep. Talk soon.
